library_integration.source: How to Make Money in Stocks / 24 Essential Lessons (William O'Neil)
curriculum_points: C: Current Earnings (Minimum 18-20% increase)
A: Annual Earnings (25% growth over 3 years)
N: New (Product, Management, or Price Highs)
S: Supply and Demand (Volume at breakout)
L: Leader or Laggard (Relative Strength Index > 80)
I: Institutional Sponsorship (Look for 'Smart Money')
M: Market Direction (Identifying follow-through days)
library_integration.test_scenario: User is shown a chart with 10% earnings growth and an RSI of 40. Question: Does this meet CAN SLIM criteria? (Answer: No).

library_integration.source: How I Made $2 Million in the Stock Market (Nicolas Darvas)
curriculum_points: Price Boxes: Defining clear ceilings and floors
The Ghost Stop: Trailing stop-loss logic
Volume spikes as 'Price Igniters'
Ignoring 'Street Rumors' in favor of price action
library_integration.test_scenario: A stock breaks out of a $50-$55 box to $56 but volume is lower than average. Is this a buy? (Answer: No, lacks volume confirmation).

curriculum_points: The 'Local Advantage': Investing in what you see and use
Categorization: Slow Growers, Stalwarts, Fast Growers, Cyclicals, Turnarounds, and Asset Plays
The PEG Ratio: Price/Earnings to Growth consistency
library_integration.test_scenario: Identify if a company with a P/E of 20 and a growth rate of 5% is a 'Lynch Buy'. (Answer: No, P/E exceeds growth rate).

curriculum_points: Risk Control: Never risking more than 1-2% of total equity on one trade
Emotional Discipline: The ability to do nothing when the market is sideways
Methodology Consistency: Sticking to one 'edge'
library_integration.test_scenario: A user has lost 3 trades in a row and wants to double their position to 'get even'. Which Wizard principle is being violated? (Answer: Risk Management/Emotional Discipline).

library_integration.source: A Mathematician Plays the Stock Market (John Allen Paulos)
curriculum_points: The Gambler's Fallacy in trading
Understanding Expected Value vs. Winning Percentage
The Random Walk Hypothesis vs. Technical Analysis

library_integration.source: Lessons from the Greatest Stock Traders / Soros Biography
curriculum_points: George Soros's Reflexivity: How bias affects fundamentals
Jesse Livermore's 'Pivotal Points'
Pyramiding: Adding to winners, never to losers
